RULING - A R G U E D. The Court addresses the peremptory challenge and shares its views. Plaintiff argues. Defendant responds, judgment in Alameda County is final. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF CHEW KAM KUNG's Motion To Vacate From Order Under CCP 473(b).

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Chew Kam Kung's peremptory challenge of Judge Schulman, filed July 23, 2019, is denied. This motion seeks to vacate a May 9, 2019 order by Judge Schulman. Because Plaintiff's motion to vacate that order is a continuation of the prior proceeding, the peremptory challenge is stricken. (See Jacobs v. Superior Court (1959) 53 Cal.2d 187, 192 [a peremptory challenge "cannot be entertained as to subsequent hearings which are a part or a continuation of the original proceedings"]; Pickett v. Superior Court (2012) 203 Cal.App.4th 887, 893 [rule applies in "a subsequent proceeding, the gravamen of which is rooted in, or supplementary to, the initial proceeding"].)

Plaintiff Chew Kam Kung's motion to vacate the Court's May 9, 2019 order overruling in part and sustaining in part defendant's demurrer to third amended complaint is denied. Although Plaintiff asserts that the basis for the motion is excusable mistake, he has not made any factual showing or presented any legal argument warranting relief.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-09-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Chew Kam Kung, Telephone 415-238-1998, appearing on his own behalf as Pro Per Plantiff; (CourtCall)
John Chu, Esq., Corporate Counsel Law Group, LLP., Telephone 415-989-5300, appearing for Defendant Jincong Zhen, et al;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JINCONG ZHEN, SAM WING CONSTRUCTION, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendants Jincong Zhen, Sam Wing Construction, Inc.'s demurrer to the third amended complaint by plaintiff Chew Kam Kung is overruled as to the third cause of action for construction defects against Mr. Zhen only; and sustained without leave to amend as to the first, second, fourth, and fifth causes of action for breach of contract, unlicensed contractor, slander of title, and petition to remove mechanics lien.

As the court previously held in ruling on the demurrer to the first amended complaint, Mr. Kung has pled a viable claim for negligence (construction defects) against Mr. Zhen which needs to be restated verbatim in the operative pleading and not incorporated by reference. The new allegation against Defendant Sam Wing Construction, Inc. that it installed 20-year-life roof shingles rather than 30-year-life shingles as "agreed on" (TAC 29) is a disguised claim for breach of an oral contract that is barred by the statute of limitations; accordingly, the demurrer of Sam Wing Construction to that cause of action is sustained without leave to amend.

The breach of contract cause of action against both defendants based on an written 2010 contract and an oral 2012 contract is barred by the applicable four- and two-year statutes of limitations respectively (Code Civ. Proc. 337(a), 339), because the alleged breach, the failure to finalize work, occurred by March 2010 (TAC 13, 22).

The cause of action for "unlicensed contractor" is time-barred on its face. Any claim Mr. Kung might have under Bus. & Prof. Code 7031 to recover compensation paid to unlicensed contractors is barred by the four-year statute of limitations. (Code Civ. Proc. 343; TAC 13 [alleging last check was given to Sam Wing Construction, Inc. on February 27, 2012].)

The facts alleged do not support a viable slander of title claim. The statute of limitations has run on the mechanics lien recorded in 2012. (Code Civ. Proc. 338(g).) Further, there is a court judgment ordering foreclosure on the lien (RJN, Exh. D), and the levy by the Alameda County Sheriff, although recorded in 2019, cannot support a slander of title because it is a levy in execution of a sale for real property pursuant to a judgment. (See Deutsche Bank National Trust Co. v. Pyle (2017) 13 Cal.App.5th 513, 528 n.7 ["The elements of a cause of action for slander of title are (1) a publication, which is (2) without privilege or justification and thus with express or implied malice, (3) false, either knowingly so or made without regard to its truthfulness and (4) causes pecuniary loss."].)

The cause of action for a petition to remove mechanics lien pursuant to Civil Code 8480(a) fails to state a cause of action because defendants did commence an action for foreclosure on the mechanics lien and the Alameda Superior Court issued a judgment in their favor on Nov. 12, 2013. (RJN, Exh. D). The cause of action is barred by res judicata.

Because Mr. Kung has not shown how an amendment could cure the deficiencies in the first, fourth and fifth causes of action, the demurrer as to these causes of action is sustained without leave to amend.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-14-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS JINCONG ZHEN AND SAM WING CONSTRUCTION, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Defendants Jincong Zhen and Sam Wing Construction, Inc.'s demurrer to all four causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Chew Kam Kung is overruled as to the third cause of action for construction defects against Mr. Zhen and sustained with thirty days leave to amend as to the first, second and fourth causes of action for breach of contract, unlicensed contractor and slander of title against both Mr. Zhen and Sam Wing and the third cause of action against Sam Wing. Liberally construed, Mr. Kung's third cause of action for construction defects alleges a viable negligence claim against Mr. Zhen. Mr. Kung is given one last opportunity to allege any other valid claims he believes he has against Mr. Zhen and Sam Wing. Mr. Kung should review the April 25, 2018 order as to what he must allege for a viable breach of contract claim. For an unlicensed contractor claim, Mr. Kung must allege the construction work requiring a license that he claims was performed by an unlicensed contractor, who was the unlicensed contractor who performed the work and what damages he is seeking. Mr. Kung's third cause of action does not allege any negligent conduct by Sam Wing. For a slander of title claim, Mr. Kung must allege what actions constitute a slander of title. While it appears that Mr. Kung might be able to validly allege all of the claims he wishes to pursue against Mr. Zhen and Sam Wing, Mr. Kung's apparent difficulties with writing in English appears to be an obstacle to what he is seeking to achieve. The court suggests that, if Mr. Kung is unable to find an attorney to represent him, perhaps he might seek the help of someone who can help him with writing in English. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk
